narrative:
Quillbrook Appraisal Partners is a five-person commercial real-estate
appraisal practice: one Managing Partner who signs everything a client
relies on, three appraisers who walk the properties, and an Office Manager
who keeps the books tighter than most firms twice the size. Clients are
lenders and property holders who need defensible valuations, so the
documents read accordingly: engagement letters where the commercial terms
are executed on the signature page rather than recited in the body,
kickoff memos that list the parcels in scope, working-session minutes kept
as undated field records (the file name carries the session date, the way
the firm's document management system requires), and status reports a
credit committee can file without edits. Tone is measured and precise;
numbers appear only where the record requires them, names appear in full
on anything with a signature line.
instructions:
Author every document briefed in `docs`, as JSON matching the deliverable
schema:

  {"schema_id": "orgsmith/authoring-deliverable@1",
   "work_order_id": "wo:author:0002",
   "docs": [{"schema_id": "orgsmith/docir@1", "doc_id": "d:....",
             "blocks": [...]}, ...]}

Block kinds: heading (text, level), paragraph (text), list (items),
table (header, rows), sigblock (signers = person ids from the brief).

Hard rules:
- One DocIR per brief; doc_ids must match the work order exactly.
- Every fact id briefed for a doc MUST appear as a literal placeholder
  {{fact:<id>}} at least once in that doc's text. You do not know fact
  values; never write a number, date, or name in place of a placeholder.
- Use only placeholders for fact ids briefed on that document.
- Do not invent people, organizations, addresses, amounts, or dates.
  People and their titles come from the briefs; the org narrative sets
  tone only.
- Every entry in a doc's `mentions` list must appear VERBATIM (the exact
  `surface` string) somewhere in that doc's text. Sigblock signers count
  as mentions of themselves. Short surfaces are nicknames: work them into
  prose naturally ("...as {{nickname}} noted..."), alongside, not instead
  of, the person's full name.
- Write plain, era-appropriate business prose in the org's voice.

docs.title: Firm Overview

authors.name: Keith Wilson
authors.title: Managing Partner
authors.persona: Keith Wilson treats his signature as the firm's product and edits accordingly, reading every engagement letter and status report as though a lender's counsel will. He came up through fieldwork and still distrusts any conclusion he cannot trace back to a walked property or a documented comparable. In writing he is spare and formal, favoring short declarative sentences and striking any adjective that a credit committee might question.
